items.prompt: Which capability is a primary goal of dataset version control?
choices: Tracking changes, enabling rollback, and reproducing prior experiments
Training models without recording data versions
items.explain: History, rollback, reproducibility, and collaboration are the core goals.

items.prompt: Traditional filename versioning is usually sufficient for large, frequently changing collaborative datasets.
items.explain: Manual naming lacks automation and reliable history and becomes error-prone at scale.

items.prompt: How does DVC typically handle large datasets relative to Git?
choices: It forces all binary bytes into every Git clone
It stores data outside Git while tracking versions with lightweight references
items.explain: DVC keeps pointers in Git and data content in local or cloud storage.

items.prompt: When is Example 8.20’s plain Git CSV commit pattern most appropriate?
items.rubric: Small or modest text-like datasets whose changes sit beside code.
items.sample_answer: When the CSV stays modest in size and updates alongside analysis code in Git.
